rationale: The page contains significant information on Fiscal Federalism in India, including constitutional provisions, recommendations of the 15th Finance Commission, and details about the Finance Commission itself. This is highly relevant for polity and governance questions.
cleanedText:
Judicial Pronouncement related to Reorganisation of State
Berubari Union case, 1960: Supreme Court held that power of Parliament to diminish the area of State (under Article 3) does not cover cession of Indian Territory to a foreign country.
• Indian Territory can be ceded to a foreign state only by amending Constitution under Article 368.
• Consequently, 9th Constitutional Amendment Act (CAA), 1960 was enacted to transfer certain territory to Pakistan.
6.2. FISCAL FEDERALISM
Why in the news?
Former RBI Governor Duvvuri Subbarao underscored the need for a cooperative approach to India’s fiscal federalism.
Constitutional Provisions defining India's Fiscal Federalism
- Seventh Schedule: Delineates tax bases between Union and States (Article 246).
- Distribution of Revenue: Taxes wholly assigned to states (Article 269).
- GST Distribution: Distributed between Centre and States (Article 269-A).
- Taxes levied and collected by Union: Distributed between union and states on Finance Commission Recommendations (Article 270).
- Grants-in-Aid: Provided to states by Centre (Article 275).
- Borrowings (Union): Union government can borrow within or outside country (Article 292).
- Borrowings (State): State government can borrow within India (Article 293).
Basis of Devolution (15th Finance Commission recommendations)
• Vertical Devolution: States share from divisible pool stands at 41% based on recommendations of 15th FC.
• Horizontal Devolution: As per 15th FC, assigned criteria with their weights are: Income Distance (45%), Area (15%), Population, 2011 (15%), Demographic performance (12.5), Forest and Ecology (10%), and Tax and fiscal efforts (2.5%).
• Education: Recommended grants of Rs. 4,800 crore (Rs. 1,200 crore each year) from 2022-23 to 2025-26 for incentivising the States to enhance educational outcomes.
• Agriculture: Recommended that Rs. 45,000 crore be kept as performance-based incentive for all the States for carrying out agricultural reforms.
• Total: Aggregate transfers to States is estimated to remain at around 50.9 per cent of the divisible pool during 2021-26 period.
About Finance Commission
• Constitutional body established under Article 280 by President every five years.
• Functions: Make recommendations regarding devolution of taxes between Center and States from divisible pool which includes all central taxes excluding surcharges and cess.
• Review state of finances of Union and States.
